title: Add Town Office Staff Dashboard for Viewing Problem Reports

requirements.summary: Create TownOfficeDashboardPage to display all submitted problem reports with admin-only access
acceptanceCriteria: The page fetches and displays all submitted problem reports using the existing `useGetProblemReports` hook.
Each report card shows problem type, location, description, reporter name, reporter contact info, and submission date.
Admin users can delete individual reports from this page.
Non-admin or unauthenticated users see a restricted-access message instead of the reports.
The page is styled consistently with the existing Northern Maine wilderness theme.

file_operations.description: Create a new admin-only dashboard page that displays all problem reports fetched via the existing useGetProblemReports hook. Use the useIsAdmin hook (following the pattern in ReportProblemPage.tsx and EventsPage.tsx) to check admin status. If not admin, display a restricted-access message. For admin users, render a list of report cards showing: problem type (mapped from enum to human-readable text), location description, detailed description, reporter name, reporter contact info, and submission date (formatted from Time). Include a delete button on each card that calls the useDeleteProblemReport mutation. Style the page with the Northern Maine wilderness theme using forest greens, slate grays, and amber accents, following the card/section layout patterns from ServicesPage.tsx and ReportProblemPage.tsx. Add a prominent heading 'Town Office — Problem Reports Dashboard'. Handle loading and error states appropriately.

acceptanceCriteria: The route is registered in `frontend/src/App.tsx` using the same pattern as other existing routes.

file_operations.description: Import TownOfficeDashboardPage from './pages/TownOfficeDashboardPage'. Add a new route definition at path '/town-office/reports' that renders TownOfficeDashboardPage, following the exact same Route pattern used for existing pages like EventsPage, ServicesPage, and ReportProblemPage (wrapped in the shared Layout with Navigation and Footer).

requirements.summary: Add Town Office Dashboard navigation links to Navigation.tsx and Footer.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Town Office Dashboard' (or 'Staff Dashboard') link appears in both the desktop and mobile navigation menus.
A corresponding quick link is added to the Footer.
Link patterns are consistent with existing navigation items.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Town Office Dashboard' link to both the desktop menu (in the flex container with other navigation links) and the mobile menu (in the mobile drawer navigation list). The link should point to '/town-office/reports' and follow the same styling pattern as existing navigation links. Optionally, conditionally render this link only when the user is authenticated as admin by using the useIsAdmin hook (if this pattern is already present in Navigation.tsx for other admin-only links, follow it; otherwise render unconditionally).

Add a 'Town Office Dashboard' quick link to the footer's quick links section, placing it alongside existing quick links like 'Local Businesses' and 'Report a Problem'. The link should point to '/town-office/reports' and follow the same styling and layout pattern as other footer quick links.
